from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a readable sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    parts: list[str] = []
    for t in triples:
        s, p, o = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object

        # Specific phrasing for common airport predicates
        if p == "cityServed":
            parts.append(f"{s} serves the city of {o}")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevel":
            # Check if unit is in the object value
            if "metre" in str(o).lower() or "meter" in str(o).lower():
                parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} above sea level")
            elif "foot" in str(o).lower() or "feet" in str(o).lower():
                parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} above sea level")
            else:
                parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInFeet":
            parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} feet above sea level")
        elif p == "elevationAboveTheSeaLevelInMetres":
            parts.append(f"{s} is at an elevation of {o} metres above sea level")
        elif p == "location":
            parts.append(f"{s} is located in {o}")
        elif p == "operatingOrganisation":
            parts.append(f"{s} is operated by {o}")
        elif p == "runwayLength":
            # Check if unit is in the object value
            if "metre" in str(o).lower() or "meter" in str(o).lower():
                parts.append(f"{s} has a runway length of {o}")
            elif "foot" in str(o).lower() or "feet" in str(o).lower():
                parts.append(f"{s} has a runway length of {o}")
            else:
                parts.append(f"{s} has a runway length of {o} metres")
        elif p == "runwayName":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s runway is named {o}")
        elif p == "country":
            parts.append(f"{s} is in {o}")

            parts.append(f"{s} is part of {o}")

        elif p == "leaderParty":

            parts.append(f"{s} is owned by {o}")

        elif p == "language":
            parts.append(f"{s} speaks {o}")
        elif p == "officialLanguage":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s official language is {o}")
        elif p == "mayor":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s mayor is {o}")
        elif p == "largestCity":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s largest city is {o}")
        elif p == "countySeat":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s county seat is {o}")
        elif p == "jurisdiction":
            parts.append(f"{s} has jurisdiction over {o}")
        elif p == "demonym":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s people are called {o}")
        elif p == "currency":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s currency is {o}")
        elif p == "city":
            parts.append(f"{s} is located in {o}")
        elif p == "headquarter":
            # Collect all headquarter values for this subject to avoid redundancy
            if 'headquarters' not in locals():
                locals()['headquarters'] = {}
            if s not in locals()['headquarters']:
                locals()['headquarters'][s] = []
            locals()['headquarters'][s].append(o)
            # Skip adding to parts now, will be handled later
            continue
        elif p == "regionServed":
            parts.append(f"{s} serves the region of {o}")
        elif p == "hubAirport":
            parts.append(f"{s} has hub airport at {o}")
        elif p == "foundedBy":
            parts.append(f"{s} was founded by {o}")
        elif p == "foundingYear":
            parts.append(f"{s} was founded in {o}")
        elif p == "postalCode":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s postal code is {o}")
        elif p == "areaCode":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s area code is {o}")
        elif p == "ceremonialCounty":
            parts.append(f"{s} is in ceremonial county {o}")
        elif p == "administrativeArrondissement":
            parts.append(f"{s} is in administrative arrondissement {o}")
        elif p == "chief":
            parts.append(f"{s}'s chief is {o}")
        elif p == "class":
            parts.append(f"{s} belongs to class {o}")
        elif p == "division":
            parts.append(f"{s} belongs to division {o}")

            parts.append(f"{s} belongs to order {o}")
        elif p == "aircraftHelicopter":

        elif p == "transportAircraft":
            parts.append(f"{s} uses transport aircraft {o}")
        elif p == "aircraftFighter":
            parts.append(f"{s} uses fighter aircraft {o}")
        elif p == "attackAircraft":
            parts.append(f"{s} uses attack aircraft {o}")
        elif p == "battle":
            parts.append(f"{s} was involved in the battle of {o}")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthFeet"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            # Convert ordinal to word for better readability
            ordinal_map = {"1st": "first", "2nd": "second", "3rd": "third", "4th": "fourth", "5th": "fifth"}
            rn_word = ordinal_map.get(rn, rn)
            parts.append(f"{s}'s {rn_word} runway has a length of {o} feet")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayLengthMetre"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            # Convert ordinal to word for better readability
            ordinal_map = {"1st": "first", "2nd": "second", "3rd": "third", "4th": "fourth", "5th": "fifth"}
            rn_word = ordinal_map.get(rn, rn)
            parts.append(f"{s}'s {rn_word} runway has a length of {o} metres")
        elif p.endswith("RunwaySurfaceType"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            # Convert ordinal to word for better readability
            ordinal_map = {"1st": "first", "2nd": "second", "3rd": "third", "4th": "fourth", "5th": "fifth"}
            rn_word = ordinal_map.get(rn, rn)
            parts.append(f"{s}'s {rn_word} runway has a surface of {o}")
        elif p.endswith("RunwayNumber"):
            rn = p.split("Runway")[0].lower()
            # Convert ordinal to word for better readability
            ordinal_map = {"1st": "first", "2nd": "second", "3rd": "third", "4th": "fourth", "5th": "fifth"}
            rn_word = ordinal_map.get(rn, rn)
            parts.append(f"{s}'s {rn_word} runway is numbered {o}")
        else:
            # Generic fallback - avoid REDACTED_TOKEN
            clean_p = p.replace("<REDACTED_TOKEN>", "").strip()
            if clean_p and clean_p != p:
                parts.append(f"{s}'s {clean_p} is {o}")
            else:
                parts.append(f"{s} {p} {o}")

    # Handle headquarter consolidation
    if 'headquarters' in locals():
        for s, locations in locals()['headquarters'].items():
            if len(locations) == 1:
                parts.append(f"{s}'s headquarter is {locations[0]}")
            else:
                # Consolidate multiple locations into one statement
                if len(locations) == 2:
                    parts.append(f"{s}'s headquarter is {locations[0]} and {locations[1]}")
                else:
                    parts.append(f"{s}'s headquarter is in {', '.join(locations[:-1])} and {locations[-1]}")

    # Remove duplicate parts to avoid redundancy
    seen = set()
    unique_parts = []
    for part in parts:
        if part not in seen:
            seen.add(part)
            unique_parts.append(part)
    parts = unique_parts

    # Build a complex sentence by chaining related information with proper connectors
    if len(parts) == 0:
        sentence = ""
    elif len(parts) == 1:
        sentence = parts[0] + "."
    elif len(parts) == 2:
        sentence = parts[0] + ", and " + parts[1] + "."
    else:
        # Create a more complex sentence structure with proper connectors
        # Use "which" and "where" to create relative clauses for better flow
        sentence = parts[0]
        for i, part in enumerate(parts[1:], 1):
            if i == len(parts) - 1:
                # Last part - use "and" before it
                sentence += ", and " + part
            else:
                # Middle parts - use semicolons with "which" for relative clauses
                sentence += "; " + part
        sentence += "."

    # Ensure the first character is capitalised
    if sentence:
        sentence = sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    return sentence

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
